summary: Linked the Master Hand laser item unit by adding the missing `ItemStateTable it_803F9378` data object in `itmasterhandlaser.c` and flipping that file from `NonMatching` to `Matching` in `configure.py`. The decomp.dev report showed one new `.data` match for `main/melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser`, moving that unit's `.data` from 0.00% to 100.00%.
what_changed: `src/melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser.c` gained a global `ItemStateTable it_803F9378[]` with one state entry.
The added state table uses state id `0` and the existing callbacks `itMasterhandlaser_UnkMotion0_Anim`, `itMasterhandlaser_UnkMotion0_Phys`, and `itMasterhandlaser_UnkMotion0_Coll`.
decomp.dev reported linked code +1952 bytes, matched data +16 bytes, linked data +56 bytes, and one new `.data` match for `main/melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser`.
smart_moves: The PR made the minimum data addition needed before marking the object as `Matching`, keeping the linkage change small and easy to verify.
The added `ItemStateTable` reused already-present motion/physics/collision callback names, avoiding speculative function work.
The table layout followed the expected item state table callback order: animation, physics, collision.
The author validated the linkage through the normal configure classification path rather than leaving the object linked only locally.
decomp_lessons: For item units, a missing small `.data` state table can be enough to keep an otherwise ready source file classified as `NonMatching`.
When linking an item object, check for global `ItemStateTable` data near the top of the item source before the helper/static functions.
After reconstructing data, update `configure.py` from `NonMatching` to `Matching` only when the unit actually links and decomp.dev confirms the match.
Small data matches are still useful progress: this PR contributed only +16 bytes of matched data for the unit but allowed the object to be linked as matching.
naming_conventions: The item source path uses lowercase item naming: `src/melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser.c`.
The data symbol uses an address-derived name: `it_803F9378`.
Callbacks use the item-prefixed mixed-case convention: `itMasterhandlaser_UnkMotion0_Anim`, `itMasterhandlaser_UnkMotion0_Phys`, `itMasterhandlaser_UnkMotion0_Coll`.
Build configuration entries use `Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/<unit>.c")` once a unit is linked and matching.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Recovered a single-entry `ItemStateTable` with the exact callback pointer sequence needed for the unit's `.data`.
Converted the object classification in `configure.py` only after adding the missing data symbol.
Used the decomp.dev percentage report as confirmation: `.data` for `main/melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser` changed from 0.00% to 100.00%.
review_feedback: No human review comments were captured in the PR slice.
The only captured feedback was the decomp.dev bot report confirming one new `.data` match and overall linked/matched byte gains.
follow_up_queries: Find other item units still marked `Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/...`)` that may only be missing `ItemStateTable` data.
Search for address-derived item state table symbols like `it_803F9378` to compare table placement and callback ordering.
Look for PRs that flip item files from `NonMatching` to `Matching` after adding small `.data` tables.
